docs.title: Technical Writing Portfolio
docs.text:
Hi, I'm David Posto, a technical writer with experience creating knowledge base articles, SOPs, troubleshooting guides, and end-user support documentation for IT service desks and healthcare workflows.

This site is a collection of writing samples that demonstrate my documentation style: clear step-by-step procedures, consistent structure, and plain language designed for front-line support staff.

docs.title: What You'll Find Here
docs.text:
This portfolio contains sample documentation demonstrating:

  • Knowledge base article development
  • Step-by-step troubleshooting procedures
  • Process and workflow documentation
  • Decision-tree based support guides
  • Documentation written for front-line support teams

docs.title: About These Articles
docs.text:
These articles are based on real help desk workflows I've documented professionally. Names, locations, and organization-specific details have been generalized to protect confidentiality.

Each article follows a structured format:

  • Symptoms — what the caller or user is experiencing
  • Solution — step-by-step procedures with decision branches
  • Escalation — what to do when standard steps don't resolve the issue

docs.title: New Patient Scheduling
docs.text:
Healthcare workflow documentation describing the process for scheduling new patients, including decision points, insurance verification, provider selection, and escalation paths.

Read Article

docs.title: ISP Static Configuration
docs.text:
IT networking documentation describing the process for verifying and reconfiguring a back office machine to test ISP static IP settings, including adapter configuration, direct ISP device testing, and escalation paths.

Read Article

docs.title: How-To: Screen-Share
docs.text:
IT support documentation describing the process for initiating a remote screen-share session with a user's computer using Endpoint Central or BeyondTrust, including VPN considerations, connection method selection, and session handoff procedures.

Read Article

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I'm based in the Indianapolis area and am currently seeking Technical Writer, Knowledge Management, and IT Documentation opportunities.

  • GitHub: github.com/dposto

docs.title: How-To: Screen-Share
docs.text:
This article provides procedures for initiating a screen-share session with a user's computer using Endpoint Central or BeyondTrust.

Author David Posto Document Type Knowledge Base Article Audience Help Desk Agents Last Updated June 2026

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text: How to initiate a screen-share session with a user's computer.

docs.title: Step 1: Important Information
docs.text:
1. ⚠️ NEVER take a screenshot of protected health information (PHI).

  2. If the device is a Mac, Apple, or mobile device:

    1. We are unable to screen-share into these devices.

    2. Note the following in the Service Order:

      1. The type of device.

      2. That the device cannot be screen-shared into.

    3. Return to the Originating Article and attempt to assist the caller as able.

  3. For non-dedicated agents:

    1. Screen-share accounts are provided to dedicated agents only.

    2. If you were prompted to initiate a screen-share, return to the Originating Article and attempt to assist the caller as able.

  4. For dedicated agents, if you receive an Admin UAC prompt, see Article 8163 to use admin credentials.

docs.title: Step 2: GlobalProtect VPN
docs.text:
1. It may be necessary to disconnect from GlobalProtect VPN to be able to perform troubleshooting steps on the user's system.

  2. In such a case:

    1. ⚠️ This code should only be entered by the dedicated agent screen-sharing into the system.

    2. Note the reason for disconnecting from GlobalProtect VPN in the Service Order.

    3. In your web browser, open your 1Password extension and enter your password.

    4. On the left side, under "All Items", select GlobalProtect Disable Passcode.

    5. Click the password field to copy the code.

      { width=600 }

    6. On the user's system, in the "Enter the passcode to disconnect" prompt, paste the code, then click Disconnect.

      { width=300 }

    7. ⚠️ You MUST reconnect to GlobalProtect VPN before exiting the screen-share.

  3. Proceed to Step 3.

docs.title: Step 3: Determine Connection Method
docs.text:
1. To initiate a screen-share with a company-supported computer, proceed to Step 4.

  2. To initiate a screen-share with a non-company-supported computer, proceed to Step 5.

docs.title: Step 4: Connect via Endpoint Central
docs.text:
1. Open a web browser and navigate to https://endpointcentral.manageengine.com/webclient#.

    { width=500 }

  2. Sign in using your "mydomain.local" username in the form of pam-wa-(USERNAME).

    1. For instructions on retrieving the "mydomain.local" username and login information from Desktop, see Article 8163.

      { width=500 }

  3. You will then be taken to the company login where you will need to sign in with the same credentials.

    { width=500 }

  4. Once signed in, select Tools at the top, then select Remote Control.

    { width=700 }

  5. Expand the window by clicking the "less than" symbol with three horizontal lines.

    { width=700 }

  6. Have the caller perform the following:

    1. On your computer, click the Windows Start button then search for and select About your PC.

    2. Please read me the name to the right of "Device name". Note the caller's response in the Service Order.

  7. In the "Devices" tab, to the right of "Total Records" click the magnifying glass symbol.

    { width=700 }

  8. Search for the computer name noted in Step 4.6.b.

    { width=700 }

    1. If still not found, proceed to Step 5 to connect via BeyondTrust.

  9. In the "Action" column, click Connect.

    { width=700 }

  10. On the "endpointcentral.manageengine.com wants to.." prompt, click Allow.

  11. On the right side of the window, in the "View" section, you should see all of the user's monitors listed.

  12. You can select any of the screens to view and control the user's system.

  13. You will also see a small notification at the bottom of the screen confirming that you are connected.

    { width=700 }

  14. Return to the Originating Article.

docs.title: Step 5: Connect via BeyondTrust
docs.text:
1. Using the Web Rep Console:

    1. In your browser, navigate to https://support.beyondtrustcloud.com/login/downloads/console.

    2. Leave "Authenticate Using" to SAML Credentials and click Login.

      { width=450 }

    3. If prompted, enter your company credentials.

    4. On the "Consoles & Downloads" page, click Launch Web Rep Console.

      { width=600 }

    5. Generate a session key by either clicking Generate Session Key under "My Queues", or by clicking Start at the top-left.

      { width=700 }

    6. Proceed to Step 5.3.

  2. Using the Desktop Representative Console:

    1. Open the BeyondTrust Remote Support application.

    2. On the login screen, leave "Authenticate Using" to SAML Credentials and click Login.

      { width=500 }

    3. On the browser page that pops up, complete the login using your company credentials.

    4. At the top-left, click Start.

    5. In the "Start Support Session" window, click Generated Session Key.

      { width=700 }

    6. Proceed below.

  3. The "Support Session Key" window will open.

  4. ⚠️ Do not send the link via email, especially if you do not have the user on the line (Callback scenarios).

    { width=450 }

  5. Instruct the user: In your browser, navigate to https://support.company.com.

  6. Provide the session key to the user and have them click Submit.

    { width=500 }

  7. Instruct the user: In the pop-up, enter your name and click Submit.

    { width=400 }

  8. The user will be listed in queue. Double-click the session.

    { width=700 }

  9. At the top, click the Web Chat tab, then click Full Customer Client.

    { width=700 }

  10. Instruct the user: On the prompt, click Accept.

    { width=450 }

  11. Instruct the user: A file will download. Click the file to run it when the download is complete.

    { width=400 }

  12. ⚠️ When you have finished with the screen-share, log out of BeyondTrust. Only 10 users can be logged in at a time.

  13. Return to the Originating Article.

docs.title: Reconfiguring FOM / WFM to Determine Center's ISP Static Configuration Status
docs.text:
This article provides procedures for determining if the static configuration is set properly in the ISP's network equipment (modem or router) using a Front Office Machine (FOM) or Wi-Fi Machine (WFM).

Author David Posto Document Type Knowledge Base Article Audience Help Desk Agents Last Updated June 2026

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text: How to determine if the static configuration is set properly in the ISP's network equipment (modem or router).

docs.title: Pre-Step 1: Set Priority
docs.text: 1. Set [Priority] to 1.

docs.title: Pre-Step 2: Important Information
docs.text:
1. ⚠️ This article is only to be used if the center is already on a backup internet connection (WAN2, hotspot, etc.) and needs to verify the ISP's static configuration before switching back to the primary connection.

  2. WAN2 on the Fortigate is a DHCP port which is typically either occupied by a cellular backup or is without a connection.

docs.title: Pre-Step 3: Verify Store has IP Information from ISP
docs.text:
1. Ask the caller: Do you have the center's IP information from their internet service provider (ISP)?

    1. If Yes:

      1. Note the following information in the Service Order:

        1. Static IP Address:

        2. Subnet Mask:

        3. Default Gateway:

    2. If No:

      1. Inform the caller:

        You will need to contact your ISP and request your Static IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway.

        When you have that information, please contact the Service Desk and reference Service Order #######.

      2. Close the Service Order.

  2. Proceed below.

docs.title: Step 1: Verify the ISP's Modem / Router is Connected to WAN2 of Fortigate and has Connectivity
docs.text:
1. ⚠️ This process is not intended for use with a cellular backup.

    1. If the store is using a cellular backup:

      1. If the store has not yet troubleshot store connectivity, see Article 4182 for instructions.

      2. If the store needs their cellular backup whitelisted, see Article 6739 for instructions.

      3. Close the Service Order.

  2. Have the caller: Please verify the network cable from your ISP's modem or router is plugged into WAN2 on the Fortigate.

  3. Wait for a few minutes for the network to come back up, then have the caller: Perform an internet search to verify the connection is working.

    1. If the connection was successful:

      1. Note that connecting to WAN2 established a connection.

    2. If the connection was unsuccessful:

      1. Inform the caller:

        We recommend a cellular backup such as the T-Mobile solution recommended by the franchisor. That can take several days to arrive.

        If you need one sooner, you can go to a different provider.

        After the cellular connection is set up, you will need to connect the cellular device to the WAN2 on the Fortigate.

      2. See Article 8524 if the caller needs instructions installing a 4G LTE / 5G connection or whitelisting a cellular backup.

      3. Close the Service Order.

  4. Proceed below.

docs.title: Step 2: Determine if Center has WFM or a FOM
docs.text:
1. Ask the caller: Are you using a WFM or FOM?

    1. If FOM, determine which port the device is located on.

      1. Have the caller:

        1. Check the front panel of the Fortigate to determine which ports (1–5) are active.

        2. Disconnect the ethernet cable from the back of the FOM.

        3. On the front panel of the Fortigate, determine which port's LEDs turned off.

        4. The set of LEDs that turned off are the port the FOM is located on.

          1. Note the port number in the Service Order.

        5. Reconnect the ethernet cable to the back of the FOM.

      2. Proceed to Step 3.

    2. If WFM, proceed to Step 3.

docs.title: Step 3: Initiate a Screen-Share Session with the FOM or WFM
docs.text: 1. See Article 3071 for instructions.

docs.title: Step 4: Configure the WFM / FOM's Ethernet Network Adapter with the Information Provided by the ISP
docs.text:
1. In the Windows Search Bar at the bottom, search for and select View network connections.

  2. Right-click the Ethernet network adapter and select Properties.

  3. Under "This connection uses the following items:" click to highlight Internet Protocol Version 4 and select Properties.

  4. Note the IP information currently in use to be used in Step 7:

    1. Static IP Address:

    2. Subnet Mask:

    3. Default Gateway:

    4. Preferred DNS:

    5. Alternate DNS servers:

  5. Change the "Use the following IP address:" to match the information provided by the ISP noted in Pre-Step 3.

  6. Change the Preferred DNS and Alternate DNS servers to appropriate servers (i.e. 8.8.8.8, 4.2.2.1).

  7. Click OK to save the changes.

docs.title: Step 5: Connect FOM / WFM to ISP's Modem / Router
docs.text:
1. Have the caller: Trace the cable plugged into WAN2 on the Fortigate to your ISP's modem / router.

  2. Ask the caller: Is the device an Optical Network Terminal (ONT or fiber modem) or does it have only one ethernet port?

    1. If Yes for either:

      1. Have the caller:

        1. ⚠️ This process will take the store's entire network down. If the store currently has customers, inform them of the following:

          1. Wait until there is a lull in customers before we proceed.

          2. If you need to, call the Service Desk back when you are not busy and reference the Service Order Number.

        2. Disconnect the WAN2 cable from your ISP's modem / router and connect your FOM / WFM to the same port on the ISP's modem / router.

        3. If the device is an ONT, remember which port it was plugged into as only that port can be used.

    2. If the device has an available ethernet port and is not an ONT:

      1. Have the caller: Plug the FOM / WFM directly into an available port on your ISP's modem / router.

  3. Proceed to Step 6.

docs.title: Step 6: Determine if FOM / WFM is Able to Access the Internet
docs.text:
1. Have the caller: Attempt to access the webpage https://speedtest.net.

    1. If site connects:

      1. Note results after running a speed test:

        1. Download Mbps:

        2. Upload Mbps:

      2. Proceed to Step 7.

    2. If site does not connect:

      1. Have the caller: Copy down the error that was received.

      2. Inform the caller: Someone at the store needs to reach out to your primary ISP informing them of the static IP configuration issue.

      3. Proceed to Step 7.

docs.title: Step 7: Return WFM / FOM Ethernet Adapter Settings to Original Settings
docs.text:
1. Initiate a screen-share with the FOM / WFM if possible.

    1. If the FOM / WFM has connectivity, see Article 3071 for instructions.

    2. If the FOM / WFM does not have connectivity, provide the instructions below to the caller.

  2. In the Windows Search Bar at the bottom, search for and select View network connections.

  3. Right-click the Ethernet network adapter and select Properties.

  4. Under "This connection uses the following items:" click to highlight Internet Protocol Version 4 and select Properties.

  5. Enter the IP information noted in Step 4.

  6. Click OK to save the changes.

  7. If WAN2 of the Fortigate was disconnected from the ISP device in Step 5, proceed to Step 8.

  8. If WAN2 of the Fortigate is connected to the ISP, proceed to Step 9.

docs.title: Step 8: Connect Fortigate WAN2 Back to ISP Network Device if Removed During Step 5
docs.text:
1. If WAN2 of the Fortigate was disconnected from the ISP device in Step 5:

    1. Provide the following instructions:

      1. Disconnect the FOM / WFM from the ISP's modem / router and plug it back into the port of the Fortigate it was originally connected to.

      2. Plug the network cable from WAN2 on the Fortigate back into the ISP's modem / router.

    2. Wait a few minutes for the network to come up.

      1. If the network does not come back up, move to Article 4182.

  2. Proceed to Step 9.

docs.title: Step 9: Check Connection to AMS and USM
docs.text:
1. Have the caller: Check the connection to AMS and USM to see if they are working.

    1. If both are not working:

      1. Determine the currently used Public IP address and make note of it.

      2. Proceed to Step 10.

    2. If AMS is giving a Dynamics Integration Error, move to Article 9463.

    3. If USM is not working, move to Article 2857.

  2. If the site connected in Step 6, proceed to Step 10.

  3. If the site did not connect in Step 6, close the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 10: Assign Service Order to the Appropriate Resolver Group
docs.text: 1. See Article 7614 for instructions.

docs.title: New Patient Scheduling
docs.text:
This article provides procedures for scheduling new primary care patients, including insurance verification, provider selection, appointment scheduling, and escalation scenarios.

Author David Posto Document Type Knowledge Base Article Audience Scheduling Agents Last Updated June 2026

docs.title: Symptoms
docs.text: New patient needs to schedule an appointment with a Primary Care Provider (PCP).

docs.title: Pre-Step 1: Cardiac Concerns or Shortness of Breath
docs.text:
1. If the patient at any time expresses concern with cardiac issues or shortness of breath:

    1. Inform the caller:

      Your symptoms suggest a serious condition that requires immediate medical attention.

      Please hang up the phone and call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room.

    2. Close the Service Order.

docs.title: Pre-Step 2: Addressing Sensitive Topics
docs.text: 1. For information on how to address sensitive topics during calls such as gender or diagnosis, proceed to Article 7392.

docs.title: Step 1: Determine if New or Existing Patient
docs.text:
1. Ask the caller: Are you needing to schedule an appointment with a new primary care provider?

  2. If Yes, proceed to Step 2.

  3. If the caller is currently an existing patient:

    1. Inform the caller: This number is for scheduling new patients.

    2. Ask the caller: Are you interested in scheduling with a new provider, or do you want to stay with your current provider?

    3. If the patient wants to schedule with their current provider:

      1. Note in the Service Order the caller is not a new patient.

      2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

      3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

      4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

    4. If the patient would like to schedule with a new provider, proceed to Step 2.

docs.title: Step 2: Gather Patient's Insurance and Determine if Accepted
docs.text:
1. See Article 6271 to determine if the caller's insurance is accepted.

  2. Note the caller's insurance.

  3. If accepted:

    1. Inform the patient: Your insurance is accepted.

    2. Proceed to Step 3.

  4. If NOT accepted, ask the caller: Your insurance is not currently accepted. Would you still like to schedule a new appointment?

    1. If Yes, proceed to Step 3.

    2. If No:

      1. Inform the patient: Thank you for calling. Have a great day.

      2. Close the Service Order.

  5. If the patient does not have insurance, proceed to Step 3.

docs.title: Step 3: Determine which City or Town the Patient Needs to Schedule In
docs.text:
1. Inform the caller of the available service area locations.

  2. Ask the caller: Which area do you live closest to, or where would you like to see the provider?

  3. Note the caller's response.

docs.title: Step 4: Determine which Practice to Schedule With
docs.text:
1. In a separate browser tab, navigate to the PCPs Taking New Patients & Online Scheduling reference spreadsheet.

  2. At the bottom, click on the PCPs Taking New Patients tab.

    1. The practices are the colored rows with the PCPs taking new patients listed below.

    2. Practices are grouped by city, with each city being a separate color.

  3. In column A [City / Town], locate the city or town noted by the caller in Step 3.

  4. Provide the caller the name of the practices and their location listed for the city or town to determine which the caller would like an appointment at.

    1. The practice's name is in column B [Practice / Provider].

    2. The address and location are on the same line to the right.

    3. ⚠️ The practice must have providers listed below it to be able to schedule a new patient.

  5. If you are unable to determine what practice to schedule the caller at:

    1. If current time is Monday–Friday, 8am–4:30pm:

      1. Warm transfer the caller to the Scheduling Supervisor.

      2. Close the Service Order.

    2. If outside standard hours or the warm transfer is unsuccessful:

      1. Proceed to Step 8.

  6. Note the selected practice in the Service Order.

docs.title: Step 5: Determine which Provider to Schedule With
docs.text:
1. If the caller requests a specific provider, try to schedule with that provider first.

  2. Review the providers listed under the practice selected in Step 4.

    1. If more than one provider is listed, check in column G [Scheduling Priority] if one should be picked first.

    2. If no priority is listed, any provider listed can be selected.

  3. Verify in column F [Age / Insurance] that the patient's age and insurance noted in Step 2 are accepted.

  4. In column H [Special Instructions], check for any information noted and provide it to the caller.

  5. If the caller requests information about the provider, a link to the provider's biographical information is available in column E [Provider Bio Link].

  6. Note the selected provider in the Service Order.

  7. Proceed to Step 6.

docs.title: Step 6: Select an Appointment with the Provider
docs.text:
1. For the selected provider, in column D [Online Scheduling Link], click the scheduling link to pull up the provider's available appointments.

    1. If the message "Sorry, we couldn't find any open appointments." appears:

      1. Repeat Step 5 to see if there are additional providers listed for the same practice.

      2. If no additional providers are listed for the practice, ask the caller: Would you like to schedule an appointment at a different location?

        1. If Yes:

          1. Repeat Step 4 to determine if another practice is acceptable for the caller.

          2. If another practice is found, repeat Step 5.

        2. If No:

          1. Note the following in the Service Order:

            1. The practice the patient needs scheduled at.

            2. Dates and times the caller needs.

          2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

          3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

          4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

  2. Review the appointments with the caller to determine which appointment they would like.

  3. If "more..." is below an appointment time, hover your mouse over it to view additional times available.

  4. If an appointment is not available within 7 days:

    1. Repeat Step 5 to see if there are additional providers listed for the same practice.

    2. If no additional providers have availability at the same practice within 7 days, inform the caller of the next available appointment.

      1. If the next available appointment is acceptable, proceed to Step 7.

      2. If the next available appointment is NOT acceptable, ask the caller: Would you like to schedule an appointment at a different location?

        1. If Yes:

          1. Repeat Step 4 to determine if another practice is acceptable for the caller.

          2. If another practice is found, repeat Step 5.

        2. If No:

          1. If the next available appointment is more than 2 weeks out, ask the caller:

            Would you like to schedule the appointment and be put on a waitlist if a sooner appointment opens up?

            If a sooner appointment does become available, you'll receive a text notification to your cell phone notifying you.

            1. If Yes:

              1. Note the following in the Service Order:

                1. "SMS Waitlist opt-in"

                2. Cellphone number.

              2. Proceed to Step 7.

            2. If No:

              1. Note the following in the Service Order:

                1. The practice the patient needs scheduled at.

                2. Dates and times the caller needs.

              2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

              3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

              4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

          2. If the next available appointment is less than 2 weeks out:

            1. Note the following in the Service Order:

              1. The practice the patient needs scheduled at.

              2. Dates and times the caller needs.

            2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

            3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

            4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

  5. Proceed to Step 7.

docs.title: Step 7: Gather Patient Information and Schedule Appointment
docs.text:
1. Click the selected appointment.

  2. In the pop-up, complete the CAPTCHA.

  3. Verify the provider, appointment date and time, and location listed are correct.

  4. In the "Reason for Visit" field, enter the caller's reason.

    1. For example, "New patient visit", or if the caller mentions any additional information, enter that as well.

  5. Click Continue.

  6. On the right, under "Continue as a Guest", click Continue.

  7. On the Patient Information screen, ask for and enter the patient's information.

    1. Items listed with red asterisks (*) are required.

    2. ⚠️ Do NOT request the patient's Social Security Number!

      1. For the Social Security Number field, enter either "000-00-0000" or "999-99-9999".

    3. Click Next.

  8. On the Insurance Information screen, ask for and enter the patient's insurance information.

    1. This information can be found on their insurance card for the insurance noted in Step 2.

    2. If the caller does not have insurance, select No Insurance.

    3. If the caller's insurance was not listed in Article 6271, select Not Listed.

    4. Items listed with red asterisks (*) are required.

    5. FYI: "Subscriber ID" may appear as "Subscriber Number", "Member Number", "Member ID", "Policy Number", "Policy ID", etc.

    6. Click Schedule it! to complete the appointment.

  9. Inform the caller: You will shortly receive an e-mail confirmation of your appointment, as well as an e-mail containing a link for your patient portal registration.

  10. If the caller's insurance was not listed in Article 6271:

    1. Provide the phone number to the office and offer a warm transfer if they would like to speak to someone about their insurance.

    2. If needed, see Article 5814 for instructions to warm transfer the caller.

  11. If the appointment is successfully scheduled:

    1. Note the following in the Service Order:

      1. "Appointment Scheduled"

      2. Appointment Date.

      3. Appointment Time.

      4. Appointment Location.

    2. Close the Service Order.

  12. If unable to schedule the appointment:

    1. Note the following in the Service Order:

      1. The practice the patient needs scheduled at.

      2. Dates and times the caller needs.

      3. Any errors or issues.

    2. Determine if the practice is open and if so, warm transfer the caller.

    3. See Article 5814 for instructions.

    4. If the office is currently closed, proceed to Step 8.

docs.title: Step 8: Escalation
docs.text:
1. If the issue remains unresolved, follow the appropriate escalation procedure.

  2. See Article 3956 for instructions.
